had a two-year-old daughter and I wanted to just get back to building because it was really energizing to me.

thinking about the inputs.

Well, guess what?

couldn't parallelize things.

And so very quickly, with Daniel's background and having built if, if this and that, with the distributed infrastructure serving, you know, billions and billions of workflow runs a month, and, you know, thousands of integrations, he quickly kind of went back to those basics of, like, how do we build this in code?
Because code is what works and what you can run.
And, and so as we started that journey, what we realized is that coding agents are just so much better.

And the reason coding agents are so good is because you have all the messy middle, all the data, all the, you know, the source code, you can test the source code, you can see the pull requests, you can see the comments, you can see the version control, you can see the documentation is really well documented.
And it's been trained on years and years and, you know, a ton of that data that's.
Really well structured.

And so they're getting better, faster and faster, right?
So then what we did is like we took that and we said, let's apply coding agent to building workflows, which is way, way, way better than a local tool.

And then how do you describe that work, and then how do you start to run that work and start to observe that work and understand where are the human interventions important, where are they at value, where can experts architect these thought processes, and they're going to be different again and again, but if you can do it in code with coding agents, it can actually be built and...

need people that are the best at their craft.

Really focused on content.

And so by getting content and being really good at content, this can translate to a lot of other things that we already started to see.

Us, like to do the interventions, how do you evaluate those interventions, how you give the right context to us humans, right?

And you do that and you validate, like that's really useful, both for improving the outcome, but also for us to learn next time around, how do we make sure that, you know, our workflows are delivering on that?

No, there's a lot of it is happening in isolation today, right?
And so part of it is what we built first is the workflow engine, and we were using AeroOps to trigger the workflows and be more of the interface to run a lot of this work.
We're using AeroOps as kind of like the database that customers see that's kind of tracking the work and all the content we're working on, the stages, the status.
And we're using Notion as kind of like the hub where all the documentation, everything we're learning about them, the meetings we have, the results, everything.
And Slack for real-time communication, right?
So that's still true for all our customers.
And then we get access to their tools.
Like, let's.
They search console, we pull data, we maybe build a Looker dashboard and share the data with them, right?

And so what the content OS will do, we'll figure out the right name to call it, is essentially have a lot of what's happening in, you know, AirOps, some of what was happening in Notion, some of what was happening in Airtable, and some of what was being edited in Google Doc, and put it all in one place so that we can actually start to collect that data and track that data, right?
Because right now, if you copy and paste an output from AirOps, and then into a Google Doc, and then you're doing some line editing, right?
And then the client goes in there, leaves a comment, and then you take that comment, you tweak it, and maybe you throw it back into another workflow, or you go into ChatGPT and tweak it.
It's like, we're losing all of that, right?

So the goal was, like, part of moving into our platform and having people do all the work in there is to get to that place, where we're first, like, doing all the work in one place, then collecting all the right data, and then starting to find ways to...
To learn from it and parallel building the task hub that will orchestrate the work, right?

And part of the orchestrating the work too is like, it doesn't always make sense to have every piece of the human interventions be done by one person that is like a full-time employee that has all this context, right?
Sometimes you need an expert, like maybe you need a security expert to review something for a normal security.
Are you going to go hire a very expensive, really hard-to-find security expert to just do a review on one piece of the content for a normal security?
Probably not, right?
So then it's like, well, you want to learn from that expert so that next time around, hopefully, you can make that human intervention easier, better, faster, you know?

It looks pretty clean and decent, right?
So here's like other ones, like here's a website, you know, again, like, so the idea is like, like, you know, this works, right?
It's not crazy, but it works.

And there is also like so many things that we build.

An app that we want to launch it, you know, one off, but it's like a workflow, essentially.
So it's almost like code is being replaced by workflows.
You know, these workflows might have some agentic steps to it, but they do so much.
And so with way fewer lines of code, you can achieve things that used to take maybe millions of lines of code before, right?

And so I want to see here.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Onboard new team members and provide a high-level overview of GrowthX's vision, strategy, and AI-driven workflow approach.](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=326.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX leverages AI workflows to replicate expert thought processes, aiming to deliver high-quality content at scale](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
  - [The company is transitioning from third-party tools (e.g., Aerox) to its own ContentOS platform for improved workflow management and learning](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1311.0)
  - [Service delivery helps GrowthX understand and refine what it takes to produce excellent outcomes, informing product development](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1357.0)
  - [The company sees potential to expand beyond content into other knowledge work domains](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=2098.0)

## Topics

### Company Background and Vision

  - [Marcel's background: CMO experience, focus on organic growth through value delivery](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=468.0)
  - [GrowthX started by distilling marketing workflows into AI-driven processes](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=443.0)
  - [Goal: Help brands become more like publishers, building "authority funnels" instead of sales funnels](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
  - [Key skills for AI-native work: thinking in first principles and effective delegation](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=524.0)

### AI Workflow Engine

  - [Transition from low-code tools to code-based workflows for better management and scalability](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1821.0)
  - [Benefits: version control, parallel processing, improved testing and documentation](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1825.0)
  - [Example: Deep researcher workflow for topic analysis and competitive comparisons](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1953.0)
  - [Runtime layer handles 300,000+ workflow runs monthly, each equivalent to 1-10 hours of human work](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1013.0)

### Service Strategy and Learning

  - [Service delivery forces high-quality outcomes and expert involvement](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1097.0)
  - [Tech supports experts in delivering outcomes, creating a feedback loop for continuous improvement](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1920.0)
  - [Focus on replicating world-class work rather than entry-level tasks](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1121.0)
  - [Content workflows as a starting point, with potential applications to other knowledge work domains](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)

### ContentOS Platform

  - [In-house platform replacing third-party tools (e.g., Aerox, Notion, Airtable)](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1311.0)
  - [Centralizes workflow execution, human interventions, and data collection](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1889.0)
  - [Enables better tracking, measurement, and learning from human interventions](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1907.0)
  - [Goal: Improve editor experience and facilitate data-driven workflow optimization](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1920.0)

### Expansion Beyond Content

  - [Potential applications in web development, design, recruiting, and sales](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=2098.0)
  - [Example: AI-generated app development instructions and functioning prototypes](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=2168.0)
  - [Human expertise still crucial for quality control, customer interaction, and specialized tasks](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=2277.0)

### Client Engagement Model

  - [Introduction of "strategy sprints" led by an elite team](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=2314.0)
  - [Assesses broader opportunities beyond editorial work (e.g., Udemy course repurposing project)](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=2333.0)

## Next Steps

  - [New team members to review video library of previous workshops](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1386.0)
  - [Explore the "best hire mindset" documentation, especially for delivery team members](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=2376.0)
  - [Continue onboarding process, allowing time for information to connect (4+ days typical)](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=1397.0)
  - [Reach out to Marcel or team leads with any questions or feedback](https://fathom.video/share/oPhAfVT3q36P9KaipURNkzMpNgsRC5WD?tab=summary&timestamp=2400.0)
